overview: Only one component exists with no dependencies, so a single foundation phase is sufficient to deliver the entire system.

phases.name: Foundation Web Application
phases.description: Build the sole Go web application component that serves all endpoints.

components.name: hello-web
responsibilities: Render a server-side Hello World HTML page with optional name personalization
Serve an embedded favicon and a friendly 404 page for unknown routes
Expose /health and /status endpoints for liveness and runtime info
Apply request logging and security headers via middleware
Support graceful shutdown and recover automatically on restart
components.architectureContext: Standalone public-facing web-app exposed on port 9090 behind Kubernetes ingress with HTTPS termination. No upstream or downstream services; it directly serves end users.
keyConsiderations: Use html/template for auto-escaping the name query parameter and truncate to 50 chars
Embed favicon via //go:embed; avoid external dependencies (stdlib only)
Ensure /health responds in <500ms and handle 100+ concurrent users
Implement SIGTERM graceful shutdown and multi-stage Docker build (scratch/distroless)
Apply CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, HSTS headers on every response
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Minimal Hello World web application
servers:
  - url: /
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Render the Hello World page
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: name
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            maxLength: 50
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML greeting page
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /favicon.ico:
    get:
      summary: Favicon
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Favicon image
          content:
            image/x-icon:
              schema:
                type: string
                format: binary
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /status:
    get:
      summary: Runtime status
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Status info
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  uptimeSeconds:
                    type: number
                  version:
                    type: string
                  startedAt:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
